documentation.value: Returns a space-separated list of names of existing files that match the given glob pattern.
A file glob pattern (e.g., src/*.c, **/*.h).
Finds whitespace-separated words in text that match pattern, and replaces them with replacement. The % character acts as a wildcard.
The pattern to match (use % as wildcard, e.g., %.c).
The replacement pattern (use % to refer to the matched stem, e.g., %.o).
The text containing words to search and replace.
Prepends prefix to the front of each word in names.
The string to prepend (e.g., src/).
Space-separated list of names.
Appends suffix to the end of each word in names.
The string to append (e.g., .o).
Space-separated list of names.
Extracts all but the suffix of each file name in names.
Space-separated list of file names.
Extracts the directory part of each file name in names (everything up to and including the last slash).
Space-separated list of file paths.
Extracts the non-directory part of each file name (everything after the last slash).
Space-separated list of file paths.
Executes a shell command and returns its standard output. Newlines are converted to spaces.
The shell command to execute.
Returns all whitespace-separated words in text that match any of the pattern words. Patterns may use % wildcards.
One or more patterns to match (e.g., %.c %.h).
The text to filter.
Iterates over each word in list, setting var to the current word, and evaluates text each time. Returns the space-separated concatenation of results.
The variable name to bind each word to.
The space-separated list of words to iterate over.
The expression to evaluate for each word (reference var with $(var)).
Expands the variable's value, substituting $(1), $(2), etc. with the supplied arguments. Used to invoke user-defined functions.
The name of the variable containing the function body.
The first argument, substituted for $(1).
The second argument, substituted for $(2).
If condition is non-empty, evaluates and returns then-part; otherwise evaluates and returns else-part.
A string. Non-empty means true, empty means false.
Returned if condition is non-empty.
Returned if condition is empty.
